_comment: Canonical commitment hashes for cross-language verification. Each vector specifies a payload + nonce + declared hash function, plus the expected 32-byte commitment hash in hex. Production runtimes (Rust, TypeScript) targeting Ligate Chain MUST reproduce these hashes byte-for-byte. Per paper §3.4, the protocol-level nonce floor is 128 bits (16 bytes); these vectors all meet or exceed that floor. Hash values generated by `compute_commitment_hash` in the reference simulator and verified by re-running the test_commitment.py canonical-encoding test.
notes: Cross-language test discipline matches per-schema-fees-sim and native-delegation-sim test_vectors/ format.
Downstream Rust / TypeScript implementations should add their own vectors here verified against this canonical baseline.
